Select any section to begin. You can return to this menu at any time.

SECTION 01
Smart Tools
Step 1 of 3
Step 1 of 3

Request Smart Tools Access

Before you can provision your team, you need access to Smart Tools in Workbench. Submit a SNOW ticket to request access.

  • Navigate to Workbench and open a SNOW ticket
  • Select Smart Tools as the Request type
  • Submit on behalf of yourself as a manager
⚡ ACTION REQUIRED — MANAGERS ONLY

All managers (Technician, Commercial, Dealer, Arbitration) must complete this before provisioning their teams.

SNOW Ticket — Smart Tools Request
Step 2 of 3

Find Smart Tools in Workbench

Once your access is approved, log in to Workbench and navigate to Smart Tools from the left menu.

  • Log in to Workbench at your standard URL
  • In the left navigation, scroll to Smart Tools
  • You will now see a search interface to look up team members
WHAT YOU'LL SEE

A search bar where you enter a team member's email address, then a table showing their current product access.

Workbench — Left Navigation
Step 3 of 3

Assign PSI Access to Team Members

Search for each team member by email, then add the PSI product with the correct role.

  • Search by team member email address
  • Click + Add New Product
  • Select PSI from the product dropdown
  • Assign the Inspector role and save
💡 TIP

Repeat this for every technician, admin, coordinator, and estimator who will use PSI during the BETA.

Smart Tools — Assign PSI Role
Step 1 of 3

Open the Checkpoint App

PSI is built directly into the Checkpoint application — no separate download needed. All provisioned devices will have Checkpoint installed by your BETA start date.

  • Tap the Checkpoint icon on your device
  • You will see the Cox Automotive sign-in screen
  • Enter your Cox Automotive email address
SUPPORTED DEVICES

Samsung, ET, and TC mobile devices only. Other device types are not supported for BETA.

Checkpoint — Login Screen
Step 2 of 3

Complete SSO & Multi-Factor Authentication

Checkpoint uses Single Sign-On (SSO) — the same login you use for other Cox Automotive tools. After entering your email, you will complete Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) before accessing the app.

1
Enter Email

Type your @coxautoinc.com email address and tap Next.

2
SSO Redirect

You'll be redirected to the Cox Automotive SSO portal automatically.

3
Complete MFA

Approve the MFA prompt on your authenticator app or via SMS.

4
Access Granted

You land on the Checkpoint home screen. You're in.

Step 3 of 3

Navigate to PSI from the Home Screen

After login you'll land on the Checkpoint home screen. Tap the PSI tile to begin inspections.

  • You will only see workflows you have been provisioned for
  • Some users will see both CERT and PSI tiles
  • Others may see only one — this is expected and correct
  • Tap PSI to open the inspection scanner
💡 DON'T SEE PSI?

Contact your manager — your account may not be provisioned yet in Smart Tools.

Checkpoint — Home Screen
Step 1 of 4

Scan or Enter a Work Order

From the PSI home screen, scan the vehicle's barcode or manually type the Work Order number to begin an inspection.

  • Select your location from the dropdown at the top
  • Tap the barcode icon to scan, or tap the text field to type the WO#
  • Tap Start Inspection to proceed
  • The Recents list shows your most recent vehicles — tap any to reopen
💡 TIP — RECENTS

If another tech already started an inspection on the same vehicle, your answers will be pre-filled when you reopen it.

PSI — Scan / Work Order Entry
Step 2 of 4

Review Vehicle Info & Undercarriage

After entering a WO, the app shows vehicle details and the VCF CERT status. Review the Undercarriage Image (UCI) before starting the inspection questions.

  • Verify the vehicle description matches the unit in front of you
  • Pinch and zoom on the UCI, or tap the full-screen button for a larger view
  • Check the VCF CERT Status — this confirms a cert order exists
  • Tap Continue to proceed to the inspection checklist
⚡ NO CERT ORDER?

If no certification order is found, the app will alert you. You can still proceed — the order will be automatically logged in VCF.

PSI — Vehicle Info & UCI
Step 3 of 4

Complete the Inspection Checklist

The app guides you through each inspection section. Answer all required questions before you can finish.

  • Work through Top Check, Bottom Check, and Test Drive sections
  • Add notes to individual answers where needed
  • Use the Notes tab to leave context for coordinators
  • You can save progress and return later — all answers are preserved
  • All questions must be answered before you can tap Finish
💡 ANNOUNCEMENTS

Watch for in-app banners — they flag important alerts like "Previously Inspected" or Green Light status.

PSI — Undercarriage & F5 Notes View
Step 4 of 4

Review & Submit the Inspection

On the Review screen, confirm all inspection sections are complete, then tap Finish to submit.

  • The Review screen shows a summary of all check sections
  • Any sections with findings show a count badge
  • Set Inspection Status to Pass, Fail, or 2nd Check
  • Tap Finish — VCF updates automatically
✅ Pass

VCF CERT status → P. No further action needed.

❌ Fail

VCF → EP. Salesforce arbitration case auto-created.

🔁 2nd Check

VCF POST line → VP. Coordinator follow-up required.

PSI — Review & Finish Screen
Step 1 of 3

Understanding VP Status

When an inspection is submitted as 2nd Check, the VCF POST line updates to VP — Problem with Vehicle. VP vehicles require coordinator follow-up before they can be certified.

🔍 4T Verification

Vehicle requires a 4-point title verification before cert can proceed.

⚠️ Soft Fail

Issues were identified but the vehicle isn't a hard fail. Needs review.

💰 Needs Estimates

Damage was noted during inspection. Repair estimates must be captured.

⚡ COORDINATOR ACTION REQUIRED

VP vehicles do not resolve automatically. Coordinators must actively work through VP status vehicles using OCC and VCF reports before certification can be finalized.

Step 2 of 3

Find VP Vehicles Using Reports

Use OCC reports or VCF reports to identify all VP status vehicles at your location and organize your team's follow-up workflow.

  • Log in to checkpoint.manheim.com
  • Run the OCC report or pull a VCF CERT = VP report
  • Review the list of vehicles requiring action
  • Organize your team around these vehicles — prioritize by sale day timing
💡 TIP

During BETA, run these reports regularly — especially ahead of sale days — to prevent VP vehicles from backing up.

Checkpoint — Scan Screen (VP Vehicle Example)
Step 3 of 3

Update Inspection Status & Tech Notes

After reviewing the VP vehicle and resolving the issue, update the inspection status in Checkpoint and document the outcome in tech notes.

1
Open in Checkpoint

Log in at checkpoint.manheim.com and locate the VP vehicle by WO#.

2
Review Inspection

Review the technician's findings, F5 notes, and PSI results link.

3
Update Status

Set inspection status to PASS or FAIL based on your review.

4
Add Tech Notes

Document the outcome and any additional context in the tech notes field.

📋 WHAT HAPPENS NEXT

Pass: No further action needed.  |  Fail: Salesforce arbitration case auto-created with your tech notes and the Inspection Report link.

Knowledge Check

Answer the following questions to confirm your understanding.

QUESTION 1 OF 5
Who is responsible for provisioning team members to PSI in Smart Tools?
A The IT department submits access on everyone's behalf
B All managers — Technician, Commercial, Dealer, and Arbitration managers
C Individual employees request their own access
D The BETA support team provisions everyone automatically
QUESTION 2 OF 5
How do users log in to the Checkpoint app?
A A unique username and password created for Checkpoint only
B Single Sign-On (SSO) with Cox Automotive email + Multi-Factor Authentication
C A PIN code assigned by their manager
D Biometric login only
QUESTION 3 OF 5
What happens if a vehicle has no PSI (Cert) order when a technician tries to inspect it?
A The inspection is blocked and cannot proceed
B The app alerts the technician, allows the inspection to proceed, and automatically logs the order in VCF
C The technician must call a coordinator before continuing
D The system silently skips the vehicle
QUESTION 4 OF 5
When a technician submits an inspection as "2nd Check," what does the VCF POST line update to?
A P (Pass)
B EP (Estimate Pending)
C VP (Problem with Vehicle)
D 01
QUESTION 5 OF 5
What should a coordinator do to identify VP status vehicles requiring follow-up?
A Wait for technicians to report them verbally
B Check Salesforce for auto-created cases
C Run OCC reports or VCF CERT = VP reports in Checkpoint
D VP vehicles resolve automatically after 24 hours
🎉 MODULE COMPLETE
5/5
SCORE
Excellent Work!

You've completed the PSI in Checkpoint self-paced training. You're ready for the BETA launch on May 4, 2026.
